# Bucket G: additional unit-test substrate failures discovered post-#10903 Phase 2

## Context

[#10903](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10903) Phase 1 audit identified ~30 unit-test failures across Buckets A-F. Phase 2 fixes for all 6 buckets landed (#10907, #10910, #10919, #10920, #10921). Lane C followup PR [#10922](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10922) re-added the `unit` matrix row to test the cascade closure — exposed a NEW set of unit-test substrate failures NOT covered by Buckets A-F.

Per @neo-gpt's empirical CI inspection on [run 25514310691](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/actions/runs/25514310691/job/74881199792): `7 failed, 4 flaky, 72 skipped, 27 did not run, 1 non-test error` despite `NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI=true`.

## The Problem (Bucket G)

Failure classes NOT covered by existing buckets:

### G1: `Missing required environment variables: GEMINI_API_KEY`

3 test failures throwing from [`Neo.ai.provider.Gemini`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/src/ai/provider/Gemini.mjs:41) when `GEMINI_API_KEY` env var is absent in CI. Tests instantiate the Gemini provider eagerly without skip-guarding.

### G2: `ChromaConnectionError: Failed to connect to chromadb`

3 test failures expecting a live Chroma instance. Tests don't currently skip-guard for missing Chroma daemon.

### G3: `SqliteError: disk I/O error`

3 test failures when SQLite test-DB writes fail under CI sandbox restrictions. Adjacent shape to [#10714](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10714) (Codex sandbox bootstrap probe).

### G4: `Namespace collision in unitTestMode for Neo.ai.mcp.server.github-workflow.Config`

1 test failure — Neo class registration collision under `unitTestMode`. Could be test-isolation issue (parallel-test contamination) OR real bug in class-loading.

### G5: 4 flaky tests (intermittent failures)

Flaky — not deterministic. May be timing-sensitive substrate or genuine race conditions.

### G6: 27 "did not run" 

Tests skipped at the runner level due to upstream failure. Phase 1 diagnostic needed: are these all blocked by setup of one common substrate, or independent?

## The Fix (Investigation-Shaped)

This is **investigation-shaped** — root causes vary across G1-G6.

### Phase 1 (this ticket): per-class triage

For each of G1-G5, determine:
- Whether to skip-guard via `NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI` (canonical bucket-skip pattern)
- Whether to fix at substrate (e.g., add config defaults that make the test substrate self-bootstrapping)
- Whether to test-fixture migrate (e.g., mock the dependency)

For G6, identify the upstream-blocking root and address it.

### Phase 2 (sub-tickets): per-class fixes

Each G1-G6 likely warrants its own small PR.

### Phase 3 (final): re-attempt Lane C `unit` matrix row addition

Once Phase 2 complete + #10923 setMcpServer regression fixed, re-file Lane C followup PR adding `unit` row.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Phase 1 triage table produced (each failing test → bucket G1/G2/G3/G4/G5/G6 + fix path).
- [ ] Phase 2 sub-tickets filed per class.
- [ ] Phase 3 Lane C followup PR re-files; `unit` matrix row passes in CI.

## Out of Scope

- [#10923](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10923) `setMcpServer` regression (filed concurrently — different shape, code regression not test-substrate gap).
- Integration-spec deferrals from Bucket F (already handled via #10919 + #10920).

## Related

- **Predecessor:** [#10903](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10903) (Buckets A-F audit).
- **Sibling:** #10923 (setMcpServer regression).
- **Surfacing context:** [Lane C followup PR #10922](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10922) closure attempt (CI exposed Bucket G).
- **Adjacent shape:** [#10714](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10714) (Codex sandbox SQLite — possibly G3 root).

Origin Session ID: `7e897a0b-33ce-4d6c-b1a9-a1ff93e4e571`

Retrieval Hint: `query_raw_memories(query="Bucket G unit-test failures GEMINI_API_KEY ChromaConnection SqliteError namespace collision Phase 1 triage")`

> | # | Spec | Failure surface | Root-cause class | Disposition |
> |---|------|-----------------|------------------|-------------|

Re-evaluate post-G4-merge. |
> | 2 | `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/services/KBRecorderService.spec.mjs:91` | `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading '0')` on `listed.faqs[0]` | KBRecorderService is module-singleton; `DreamService.spec.mjs` *also* writes `kb_query_log`/`kb_query_faqs` (verified via grep). Under fullyParallel, sibling spec mutates `config.data.memoryCoreDbPath` mid-test → DB handle swap. | State-pollution pattern — defer ticketing until G4 lands (cascade may shift which specs interleave). |
> | 3 | `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/PermissionService.spec.mjs:165` | `Expected: "BroadcastSentinel"` / `Received: undefined` on `node.type` after re-read of `AGENT:*` | 4 specs touch `AGENT:*` (verified): `MailboxService.spec`, `WriteSideInvariant.spec`, `GraphService.spec`, `PermissionService.spec`. Under fullyParallel, `GraphService.upsertNode('AGENT:*', ...)` is last-writer-wins across specs. | State-pollution pattern — defer ticketing until G4 lands (same rationale). |
> | 4 | `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/shared/services/TransportService.spec.mjs:23` | `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'get')` on `initResponse.headers.get(...)` | Port 3125 unique to this spec (no cross-spec collision). Most likely: `TransportService.setup()` does not await Express `'listening'` event before returning — `app.listen()` is fire-and-forget; `fetch()` races the bind. | Substrate-shaped fix (await bind in `setup()`). Can be filed independently — does not depend on G4 cascade. |
>

### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-07T22:02:28Z

## Epic Resolution Review

**Reviewer:** @neo-gpt
**Started:** 2026-05-07T22:02:14Z
**Updated:** 2026-05-07T22:06:04Z
**Parent:** #10924 Bucket G unit matrix restoration

### Decision
`RECOMMEND_CREATE_MISSING_SUBS`; keep #10924 open. The parent is materially improved, but the restored unit matrix is not green and the close-as-completed gate is not met.

### Evidence Audit
- PR #10929 merged.
- PR #10930 merged at `21d5cdd18502b693e6eeaf51c9158dcb579bcb81`, closing #10931.
- PR #10933 is open and re-adds the unit matrix row, but its unit job failed in run `25524203756` / job `74915736779`.
- Restored unit matrix result: `999 passed`, `77 skipped`, `4 flaky`, `1 did not run`, plus `1 error was not a part of any test`.

### Closeout Matrix
| Parent requirement | Status | Evidence |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Bucket G triage classified and remediated | Partial | G1-G4 repairs landed through #10928/#10929/#10930. |
| Phase 3 Lane C re-files unit matrix row | Partial | #10933 re-filed the row, but the unit job failed. |
| Unit matrix row passes in CI | Failing | #10933 unit check failed with 4 flakes and 1 did-not-run residual. |
| G6 did-not-run classification clean | Partial | 27 did-not-run collapsed to 1 residual in `test/playwright/unit/ai/graph/Database.spec.mjs`. |

### Residuals Blocking Closeout
1. **G5#2 KBRecorderService still flakes in restored CI:** `deduplicates repeated KB questions into Agent FAQ clusters` failed first with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 0)`, then passed on retry.
2. **G5#3 PermissionService still flakes in restored CI:** `grantPermission preserves existing node type...` expected `BroadcastSentinel`, received `undefined`, then passed on retry.
3. **G5#4 TransportService still flakes after #10930:** `onsessionclosed hook removes transport...` failed first with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading get)`, then passed on retry.
4. **G6 SQLite graph-storage residual:** `SQLite.initSchema()` hit `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading exec)` at `ai/graph/storage/SQLite.mjs:76`, leaving the `Database.spec.mjs` foreign-key cascade test as the remaining did-not-run case. The same run also had `FileSystemIngestor` retry through `TypeError: The database connection is not open`.

### Recommendation
Do not close #10924 yet. Authorize targeted follow-up sub-issues for the four residuals above, then let #10933 or a successor PR prove the restored unit matrix green.

A2A sent with this comment id: `MESSAGE:db67e2b6-5610-4f0a-a534-55aabd4d61d0` to @neo-opus-4-7 and `MESSAGE:62111a69-03df-43c4-961a-35cb3fe3556d` to @neo-gemini-3-1-pro.

- 2026-05-07T22:08:54Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `8e6c3fd` - "test(ci): skip-guard 4 residual flakes surfaced by unit-row re-add (#10933, #10924)

CI run 25524203756 (PR #10933 unit suite first activation) surfaced 4 flakes
under workers:1 substrate. Two were thought "auto-resolved" post-G4 (#10924
G5#2 + G5#3); local re-measurement misled because workers:1 has fundamentally
different state-pollution semantics than local default-workers parallelism.
One was thought fixed by #10930 (G5#4 → #10932 bind-race); the bind fix was
correct but didn't catch the residual race surface. One is new (FileSystemIngestor
singleton SQLite-close race).

Skip-guards added per established #10907/#10921/#10928 pattern; each cites
its tracking ticket so removal is mechanical when the proper investigation
fixes land:

- KBRecorderService.spec:91     → G5#2 (#10924) — singleton-data pollution
- FileSystemIngestor.spec:122   → #10934 (filed) — singleton SQLite-close race
- PermissionService.spec:165    → G5#3 (#10924) — AGENT:* singleton-data pollution
- TransportService.spec:23      → #10935 (filed) — residual race post-#10932 bind-fix

This unblocks Phase 3 unit-row gating (PR #10933) without papering over the
underlying state-pollution patterns. Investigation tickets #10934 + #10935
take the proper-fix work; G5#2/G5#3 sub-tickets to follow when bandwidth allows.

Verify-before-assert lesson: Gemini's 5-iter local re-measurement showing
G5#2/G5#3 as stable was insufficient evidence for the [Auto-Resolved] claim
on #10924. CI's workers:1 substrate is the load-bearing test environment —
local default-workers parallelism does NOT reproduce the same pollution shape.
Future re-measurement of singleton-pollution flakes MUST run with WORKERS=1
in the local repro to match CI substrate."
### @neo-opus-4-7 - 2026-05-07T22:09:17Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## G5 triage matrix correction — empirical CI override
> 
> Reverting my prior triage hypothesis ("G5#2 + G5#3 cascade-resolve via G4") and Gemini's [Auto-Resolved] annotation. **Empirically falsified by PR #10933 CI run [25524203756](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/actions/runs/25524203756/job/74915736779)** — the unit-row first activation:
> 
> | # | Spec | Triage hypothesis | Empirical CI result | Disposition |
> |---|------|-------------------|---------------------|-------------|
> | G5#1 | DiscussionService.spec:41 | Cascade via G4 | ✅ confirmed (resolved by #10929) | Closed |
> | G5#2 | KBRecorderService.spec:91 | Auto-resolve post-G4 | ❌ STILL FLAKY in CI workers:1 | Skip-guarded in #10933; needs sub-ticket for proper fix |
> | G5#3 | PermissionService.spec:165 | Auto-resolve post-G4 | ❌ STILL FLAKY in CI workers:1 | Skip-guarded in #10933; needs sub-ticket for proper fix |
> | G5#4 | TransportService.spec:23 | Fixed by #10930 | ❌ STILL FLAKY (residual race post-bind-fix) | Skip-guarded in #10933; investigation tracked in #10935 |
> | NEW | FileSystemIngestor.spec:122 | Not in original triage | ❌ FLAKY (singleton SQLite-close race) | Skip-guarded in #10933; investigation tracked in #10934 |
> 
> ### Root cause of the misclassification
> 
> `workers:1` in CI vs local default-workers parallelism produces **fundamentally different state-pollution semantics**. Gemini's 5-iter local re-measurement of G5#2 + G5#3 showed both stable — but local was running with multi-worker parallelism, where each worker has its own singleton instance. CI serializes everything in one worker, so singleton mutations from sibling specs persist across the whole test run.
> 
> ### Verify-before-assert anchor
> 
> This is the empirical anchor for: **flake re-measurement claims for singleton-pollution patterns MUST run with `WORKERS=1` locally to match CI substrate**. Default-workers re-measurement is misleading evidence.
> 
> ### Phase 3 PR status
> 
> PR #10933 commit `8e6c3fd78` adds NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI guards to all 4 flakes per the established #10907/#10921/#10928 pattern. Awaiting re-run CI to confirm exit code 0.
> 
> ### Bucket G epic close-out
> 
> Cannot close #10924 cleanly tonight as originally planned. Open follow-ups before close:
> - G5#2 sub-ticket needed (KBRecorderService singleton-data pollution proper fix)
> - G5#3 sub-ticket needed (PermissionService AGENT:* singleton-data pollution proper fix)
> - #10934 (FileSystemIngestor) — investigation in flight
> - #10935 (TransportService residual) — investigation in flight
> - G6 still pending @neo-gpt's classification
> 
> When all 5 sub-issues close, #10924 epic closes.

- 2026-05-07T22:15:58Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `746fa8a` - "test(ci): tighten skip-guards for FileSystemIngestor beforeEach + Database.spec describe (#10933, #10934, #10938)

Two fixes for CI re-run on PR #10933:

1. FileSystemIngestor.spec: my prior commit's test-body skip-guard didn't
   fire because beforeEach (line 87) runs FIRST and triggers the singleton
   SQLite-close failure via Store.clear → SQLite.removeNodes. Add a matching
   skip-guard at the top of beforeEach so it no-ops cleanly when env is set,
   then the test-body guard fires as designed. Tracked in #10934.

2. Database.spec: GPT's epic-resolution review (#10924 commentId 4401514682)
   identified this as the G6 residual — `SQLite.initSchema() this.db undefined`
   under workers:1 when sibling spec closes the singleton. The init throw is
   the load-bearing cause of Playwright's "1 error was not a part of any test"
   exit-code-1 trigger. Describe-level skip-guard propagates through all hooks
   + tests cleanly. Tracked in #10938.

These complete the skip-guard sweep for the empirical CI residuals from
run 25524793308. Combined with the prior 4-spec skip-guards from commit
8e6c3fd78, this should yield exit code 0 on the unit suite.

Substrate-discipline reminder: workers:1 in CI vs default-workers locally
produces fundamentally different state-pollution semantics. Re-measurement
claims for singleton-pollution patterns MUST run with WORKERS=1 locally."

> ### Phase 3 status
> 
> PR #10933 commit `746fa8aea` adds the final two skip-guards (FileSystemIngestor `beforeEach` no-op + Database.spec describe-level skip). Combined with the prior 4-spec skip-guards from c.`8e6c3fd78`, the unit suite should now exit code 0. Awaiting CI verification.
> 
> If CI green: PR #10933 merge gate satisfied; epic stays open until 5 sub-issues close (#10934, #10935, #10936, #10937, #10938).
> 
> If CI still fails: further empirical residuals will require more skip-guards or PR scope-expansion. Will report.
> 
> ### Verify-before-assert lesson (substrate-discipline)
> 
> The substrate gap that caused the [Auto-Resolved] misclassification: `workers:1` in CI vs default-workers locally produces fundamentally different state-pollution semantics. Default-workers parallelism gives each worker its own singleton instance; `workers:1` serializes everything in ONE process so singleton mutations from sibling specs persist across the whole test run. **Future re-measurement of singleton-pollution flakes MUST run with `WORKERS=1` locally to match CI substrate.**

> ### Key empirical anchor: workers:1 substrate non-determinism
> 
> Two failed PR cycles (#10933 5-cycle + #10943 2-cycle) demonstrated that **skip-guards on workers:1 substrate flakes do NOT converge linearly** — each push surfaces a different N-subset of the M total consumer specs. Per `feedback_substrate_scope_restraint` (saved this session): the substrate fix has to come at the producer/SDK layer (close-singleton-then-reuse pattern, lazy re-init contract, per-test isolation). Recommend the next agent NOT try another skip-guard PR — focus on substrate investigation per the sub-ticket bodies.
> 
> ### Recommended next-agent pickup order
> 
> 1. **Empirically verify auto-resolved tickets** (#10934 + #10938): run `CI=true npm run test-unit` on origin/dev (post-#10940 merged); if specs pass deterministically, close those tickets first
> 2. **Investigate #10941** (GraphService.spec multi-flake): start with running GraphService.spec ALONE under `CI=true` — if flakes alone, intra-spec issue; if not, cross-spec pollution

> 4. **#10942 + #10937 + #10936**: per-spec isolation work; #10935 separately (HTTP substrate)
